title: Seven and a Half Lessons About the Brain
author: Lisa Feldman Barrett
themes: Your brain is not for thinking — it is for allostasis, keeping your body alive and healthy
The brain is not a rational thinker with emotions as add-ons — it is a prediction engine
The brain wires itself to its physical and social environment
Brains are not the product of a single evolutionary design — they are a mosaic of ancient and new systems
Your brain is uniquely yours, shaped by your specific experiences
The brain works as a network, not a collection of independent modules
excerpt: Your brain is not for thinking. It is for running your body — managing your budget of water, salt, glucose, oxygen, and other resources needed to keep you alive. Thinking, feeling, perceiving, remembering — these are secondary. The brain's primary job is allostasis: predicting and preparing for your body's needs before they arise.
